output: Executed batch 8 task `T12`. Resize state is now owned by `useClipResize`, the old `resizeStartRef` threading was removed from timeline data, `useTimelineEditing` consumes the extracted resize hook, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_8.json`.
deviations: T12 required a small follow-on edit in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts` so the removed `resizeStartRef` is no longer threaded into `useTimelineEditing` after moving ownership into `useClipResize`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Created `useClipResize` with local `resizeStartRef` ownership and moved `onActionResizeStart` / `onActionResizeEnd` into it. `useTimelineEditing` now consumes the extracted resize hook, and `resizeStartRef` was removed from `useTimelineData` so resize state is no longer shared through data composition. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "resizeStartRef|onActionResizeStart|onActionResizeEnd" ...` shows `resizeStartRef` only in `useClipResize.ts`, and `rg -n "coordinator|drag|drop|pointer|document\\.|window\\.|querySelector|classList" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts` returns no hits.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `useClipResize` now owns `resizeStartRef`, and the hook has no drag state or coordinator dependency (`rg -n "coordinator|drag|drop|pointer|document\\.|window\\.|querySelector|classList" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts` returns no hits). `rg -n "resizeStartRef|onActionResizeStart|onActionResizeEnd" ...` shows the resize ref only in `useClipResize.ts`, with `useTimelineEditing` and `useTimelineState` only consuming the extracted callbacks.
